title: Core-contaminant normalization (lead, copper, nitrate, arsenic, coliform/E. coli, TTHM/HAA5, PFAS) + non-detect/detection-limit handling
context: Accurate normalization of core regulated contaminants is the technical foundation of the explainer engine. Non-detect-as-zero is a common and harmful error in water data: a non-detect at a given reporting limit carries different meaning than a measured zero. Violation taxonomy (health-based vs. monitoring/reporting vs. treatment-technique) is critical to prevent a paperwork violation from being misread as a contamination event. The 2024 PFAS MCLs and the evolving Lead and Copper Rule are regulatory-change triggers that must be flagged for re-review.
objective: Implement normalization for all core regulated contaminants (lead, copper, nitrate, arsenic, total coliform/E. coli, TTHM/HAA5, PFAS) in pipeline/normalize/: (1) map each to a stable identity (CAS/EPA contaminant code); (2) harmonize units to a normalized form while preserving the original; (3) record the MCL and MCLG for each; (4) preserve non-detect as non-detect with the detection/reporting limit (not as zero); (5) classify violations by the taxonomy (health-based/monitoring-reporting/treatment-technique); (6) preserve resolved vs. open violation status; (7) retain sample periods distinct from publication dates; (8) flag 2024 PFAS MCLs and Lead and Copper Rule values for regulatory re-review.
acceptanceCriteria: Each contaminant normalized to a stable identity (CAS/EPA code) with harmonized units; original unit preserved
MCL and MCLG recorded for each core contaminant; sourced to EPA regulatory reference
Non-detect is preserved as non-detect (not zero) with the detection/reporting limit; unit tests cover non-detect handling for all core contaminants
Violations classified by taxonomy: health-based, monitoring/reporting, treatment-technique; resolved vs. open status preserved
Sample periods retained distinct from publication dates in the normalized schema
2024 PFAS MCLs and Lead and Copper Rule values flagged in the codebase as regulatory re-review triggers
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding pipeline/normalize/ modules for all core contaminants with unit tests for non-detect handling, violation taxonomy, and identity harmonization.
